summary: The smallest coherent DB-002 cut was to add compiler-known connection descriptors plus a Bun runtime installer, not a full SQL surface.
ergonomics: Deriving a runtime contract from `hostBindings` made the SQLite runtime helper fit the existing provider/runtime pattern cleanly.
Compiler-known builtins were easy to add in `Checker.hs`, and existing test helpers made end-to-end runtime coverage straightforward.
A field name of `path` on the builtin record tripped generated JS due to an emitter identifier collision; using `databasePath` avoided broad emitter work.
follow_ups: If SQLite handles should be non-forgeable, add an opaque/host-handle type instead of a normal record-shaped builtin.
Add first typed query/statement primitives that consume `SqliteConnection` without dropping back to ad hoc host code.
Consider exporting SQLite contract metadata from generated bindings explicitly, the same way provider/native interop surfaces are exposed.
warnings: Any tests or runtime assertions that enumerate builtin schema names will need to account for `SqliteConnection` now.
Do not name generated record fields `path` on this surface unless the JS emitter collision is fixed more generally.
`createSqliteRuntime` depends on `compiledModule.__claspAdaptHostBindings`; use it with generated modules, not arbitrary JS modules.
residual_risks: `SqliteConnection` is still a record-shaped descriptor, so it is type-checked but not opaque against user construction or forgery.
The runtime surface is Bun/Node-oriented via `node:sqlite`; equivalent helpers do not exist yet for other runtimes.
This slice only covers opening, tracking, and closing typed SQLite connections. Typed query/schema/migration surfaces still need later tasks.
